title: Rousing Spicy Tuscan Steak Tortellini
description: A spicy Tuscan-inspired brunch dish featuring lean ground beef, cheese-filled tortellini, and a zesty tomato sauce. Chill and reheat in a terracotta dish for a rustic flavor that's ready in minutes.
instructions: In a large skillet over medium-high heat, heat 2 tbsp olive oil and add 1 lb lean ground beef, cooking until browned and crumbly, about 5-7 minutes.
Stir in 3 minced garlic cloves and 1 tsp red pepper flakes, cooking for 1 minute until fragrant.
Pour in 1 cup tomato paste and 1 cup diced sun-dried tomatoes, mixing well to combine.
Add 1 tsp dried rosemary, 1/2 cup white wine, and season with salt and pepper; simmer for 5 minutes, letting the flavors meld.
While the sauce simmers, bring a large pot of salted water to a boil, add 8 oz raw tortellini, and cook until al dente, about 3-4 minutes, then drain.
Fold the cooked tortellini into the beef sauce, ensuring each piece is coated, and transfer the mixture into a terracotta baking dish.
Sprinkle 2 tbsp grated Parmesan over the top and cover loosely with foil.
Bake in a preheated 375°F (190°C) oven for 10 minutes to heat through and allow the cheese to melt.
Remove from oven, garnish with fresh basil if desired, and serve hot.
